paper_title: A note on arithmetic progressions with restricted differences
summary: Problem 8 asks whether the condition $|S|>(q+1)/2$ in Theorem 1 can be weakened; the authors note that the boundary case $|S|=\lfloor(q+1)/2\rfloor$ is already interesting, and cite Green's suggestion that the result may extend all the way down to $|S|=2$. The paper was posted on 2026-05-13 and no follow-up work addressing this specific problem has been found in the literature.
search_queries: Conlon Fox Pham arithmetic progressions restricted differences finite field 2026 arXiv
three-term arithmetic progression common difference S^n finite field cap set restricted differences 2025 2026
notes: Paper posted 2026-05-13 (one day before review date); no follow-up found. The authors cite Green's unpublished '100 open problems' manuscript as motivation for pushing down to |S|=2. A related paper (Discrete Analysis, arXiv:2308.06600) on effective bounds for {0,1,2}^n-restricted progressions addresses a specific instance of a neighbouring problem but does not resolve Problem 8 or weaken the |S|>(q+1)/2 threshold.
